description: Published anchor results for the attention-exponent census. Two anchor sets: (1) trained-model census on public checkpoints (measure_conformal_heads.py), (2) the corpus-formation ladder — 70m GPT-NeoX models trained from scratch for 2000 steps / 1.05B tokens on controlled corpora under the frozen pre-registered protocol (census_corpus.py). Formation criterion: >= 10/48 conformal heads at step 2000.

protocol.census: 50 random-token sequences (seed 42), L=512, fp32 asserted, lag profile over queries i >= max(256, dx), OLS on log A vs log dx over lags [8, 256]; conformal: R^2 >= 0.90 and Delta >= 0.05; SYK-near: |Delta - 0.25| <= 0.05
protocol.training: Pythia-70m-class GPT-NeoX (6L, 8H, d=512, ff=2048, rotary, vocab 50304), ctx 512; AdamW (0.9, 0.95, wd 0.1), peak LR 1e-3 cosine to 1e-4, 20-step warmup; batch 1024x512 tokens/step; 2000 steps = 1.05B tokens

model_census.note: Trained public checkpoints, frozen census protocol. delta_median is over the conformal (high-R^2) subset. Randomized-weight controls of the same architectures give ~zero conformal heads.

corpus_ladder.note: All rungs: identical architecture, optimizer, token budget, and census. beta_hat is the fitted exponent of the corpus's shuffle-corrected mutual-information decay MI(d) = A*d^-beta + c (free-floor estimator, exp-062 prereg). At the 70m/1B-token scale, FORMATION (n_conformal >= 10/48) is the observable; SYK-exponent maturation toward 0.25 is not yet complete at this scale (natural-text delta_median ~ 0.16-0.17), so compare your n_conformal against these anchors, not delta_median against 0.25.

anchors.corpus: C-generated (1.1B tokens generated by the run_CNAT_s0 model, temp 1.0, no truncation)
